channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Fabless chip startup backed by multi-billion Indian company wants to build a $10bn fab in India before 2027
item.description: Fabless chip startup LTSCT, backed by multi-billion Indian company, wants to build a $10bn fab facility in India.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How AI is steering the media toward a ‘close enough’ standard
item.description: The nonstop cavalcade of announcements in the AI world has created a kind of reality distortion field. There is so much buzz, and even more money, circulating in the industry that it feels almost sacrilegious to doubt that AI will make good on its promises to change the world.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: New Intel CEO Calls for Change, Urges Customers to Speak Up
item.description: Intel's new CEO Lip-Bu Tan addresses company's struggles and encourages customers to provide feedback in a keynote speech at the company's Vision conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How internet search algorithms could help critical infrastructure
item.description: Researchers found the algorithms that rank pages in internet searches also can help planners understand how to prevent failures in electrical systems. The post How internet search algorithms could help critical infrastructure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canon EOS R7 Mark II rumored for 2025 with these significant upgrades – watch out Fujifilm
item.description: Rumors suggest that Canon will announced a replacement for the EOS R7 later this year, with a 40MP sensor among the upgrades.
item.dc:creator: christian.rowlands@me.com (Chris Rowlands), TechRadar

item.title: Detailed Google Pixel 10 camera specs have seemingly leaked, pointing to an extra lens and worse sensors
item.description: Big changes could be coming to the Pixel 10's cameras, while the Pixel 10 Pro and Pro XL might not see any changes at all.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cobas Asset Management moderniza su atención al cliente con un asistente virtual impulsado por IA
item.description: Cobas Asset Management ha puesto en marcha un nuevo proyecto con vistas a mejorar la experiencia de usuario en su web y en su área privada gracias a la incorporación de un asistente virtual.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Basking in JavaScript refinements
item.description: Simple and fast. That’s all we want from our software tools. And meeting the requirements, of course. You can’t go without that. Also, we’d like them to be pleasant to use, with a good developer experience. It would be really great to have a friendly community.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Sonatype warns of 18,000 open source malware packages
item.description: Software supply chain security company Sonatype uncovered 17,954 open-source malware packages during Q1 2025, the company revealed in its Open Source Malware Index. Sonatype’s Open Source Malware Index for Q1 2025 was introduced April 2.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to accelerate digital transformation
item.description: Readers of CIO, Computerworld, CSO, InfoWorld, and Network World this week sought to learn how corporate culture can affect digital transformation, wanted to know more about the rise of cloud-based ERP, and had questions about desktop-based office software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mechanical Orchard taps gen AI for mainframe-to-cloud modernization
item.description: IBM is expected to drive AI deep into its next mainframe refresh, due this spring, in a move aimed at keeping and attracting Big Iron customers. Meanwhile, there’s no shortage of vendors vying for enterprise customers that are interested in moving off the mainframe and into the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Magid: Microsoft Turns 50
item.description: Fifty years ago, on April 4, 1975, Bill Gates and the late Paul Allen founded Microsoft. A half century later, it’s one of the world’s most valuable companies. And even though it’s based in Redmond, Washinton, it has a strong Silicon Valley presence.
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google’s new AI tech may know when your house will burn down
item.description: FireSat is a satellite project designed to detect and track wildfires early, aiming to detect a fire that's the size of a classroom within 20 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it
item.description: Want to make a Python user grind their teeth? Just recite three words: Python is slow. In many of the ways that matter, it’s true.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump to hold a meeting on possible investors to buy TikTok with possible ban at stake
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump will hold a Wednesday meeting with aides about possible investors who could buy a stake in TikTok, a deal that could potentially stop the social media site from being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Detroit production of Mozart opera turns its female characters into automatons
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Audiences at the Detroit Opera House expecting their performance of “Cosi fan tutte" to begin with the overture may be surprised to hear instead a product launch from a tech company CEO.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SILVERMAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Dow wants to power its Texas manufacturing complex with new nuclear reactors instead of natural gas
item.description: Dow, a major producer of chemicals and plastics, wants to use next-generation nuclear reactors for clean power and steam at a Texas manufacturing complex instead of natural gas. Dow's subsidiary, Long Mott Energy, applied Monday to the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission for a construction permit.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jack Black, Jason Momoa enter the Minecraft universe as the video game phenom hits the big screen
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When Jack Black got the call to star in the “Minecraft” movie, the perks were plenty. He got to work alongside Jason Momoa, reunite with “Nacho Libre” director Jared Hess and engulf himself into a beloved gaming universe.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New phishing attack uses real-time interception to bypass 2FA
item.description: This phishing kit bypasses 2FA via session hijacking and real-time credential theft. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers four ways to stay safe from Astaroth phishing attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China's telecoms giant Huawei Technologies says its profit fell 28% while revenue surged in 2024
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Chinese telecoms gear giant Huawei said Monday its net profit fell 28% in 2024 while its revenue surged, as the company invested heavily in advanced technologies.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 23andMe customer DNA ‘up for grabs’ amid bankruptcy: Attorney
item.description: With a saliva sample, DNA-testing companies like 23andMe provide a profile of a person’s genetic heritage.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
